name: Georgian

formality.description: Georgian has a T-V distinction: შენ (shen, informal/singular 'you') vs თქვენ (tkven, formal/plural 'you'). Software UI typically uses თქვენ-form for professional contexts. The distinction is similar to French tu/vous. Georgian also marks politeness through verb morphology and vocabulary choice.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Georgian has no grammatical gender. Third-person pronouns (ის/ისინი) are gender-neutral. No special inclusive conventions are needed.

professional.label: პროფესიონალური (Professional)
professional.description: Standard for software UI, websites, and business communication.
professional.prompt: Professional Georgian. Use თქვენ (tkven) for second-person address. Clear, modern language appropriate for software interfaces. Avoid overly literary or archaic phrasing. Georgian verbs are polypersonal — ensure subject and object agreement is correct.

informal.label: არაფორმალური (Informal)
informal.description: For consumer apps, social media, and casual contexts.
informal.prompt: Informal Georgian. Use შენ (shen) for second-person address. Conversational, friendly tone for consumer apps. Modern vocabulary acceptable.

notes: Georgian uses the Mkhedruli script, which has no uppercase/lowercase distinction in modern usage. The Asomtavruli (uppercase) and Nuskhuri (lowercase) scripts are historical/ecclesiastical. Georgian is the only Kartvelian language with significant digital resources.
